title: Replace better-sqlite3 with Node's built-in node:sqlite (v1.0.0)
context: better-sqlite3 was Hunch's only remaining native SQLite dependency: prebuild-install (its installer) is deprecated upstream with no fix coming, node-gyp fallback builds fail on machines without toolchains, and Windows global upgrades hit EPERM when a running node process holds better_sqlite3.node locked. The derived index uses only FTS5, WAL, recursive CTEs, and plain BLOBs — all covered by node:sqlite (unflagged since Node 22.13, verified live). Vectors were already brute-force JS, no loadable extension needed.
decision: Swap the driver in src/store/db.ts to node:sqlite DatabaseSync, loaded via createRequire with a targeted suppression of ONLY its ExperimentalWarning (hunch's stderr reaches humans, hooks, and MCP clients on every invocation). Add withTx() (BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK) since node:sqlite has no transaction() helper; type BLOB rows as Uint8Array (what node:sqlite returns). Engines floor moves >=20 -> >=22.13.0 (Node 20 is past EOL since April 2026); CI/guard/release workflows move to Node 22 so the publish-gating test run can pass. Shipped as 1.0.0 — the engines bump is breaking.
consequences: Zero install-time native builds for SQLite; ~130 fewer transitive install packages; no prebuild-install deprecation warning; no Windows EPERM on global upgrade
Node 20 and 22.0-22.12 users cannot install >=1.0.0 (engines refuses)

node:sqlite is still marked experimental in Node 22-24: API surface used (DatabaseSync/prepare/all/get/run/exec) is stable in practice, but a future Node change is absorbed in db.ts, the single wrapper
The ExperimentalWarning suppression in db.ts must stay scoped to the exact warning text — swallowing more would hide real deprecations from users
SECURITY.md: better-sqlite3 install-script and tar-stream Socket-triage entries removed; tree-sitter parsers are the only remaining native builds
alternatives_rejected: Keep better-sqlite3 and wait out the prebuild-install deprecation: upstream (12.11.1) still depends on it, no fix planned — the warning and the Windows EPERM class stay forever
Dual-driver fallback (node:sqlite with better-sqlite3 fallback for Node 20): two codepaths to test for a runtime that is already past EOL — complexity without a constituency
Suppress the ExperimentalWarning via NODE_OPTIONS/--disable-warning in a bin wrapper: not portable across Windows bin shims; in-process scoped suppression at module load is deterministic everywhere

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Keep better-sqlite3 despite upstream deprecation and native-build fragility
Wait for node:sqlite to land unflagged on Node 20 LTS (but Node 20 is past EOL; Node 22 is current LTS)
Adopt a different SQLite binding (would not solve the fundamental native-build problem class)
